name: ABS Census TableBuilder
description: The Australian Bureau of Statistics' tool for building custom tables from Census data. Essential for understanding electorate demographics — age, income, education, housing, employment, country of birth, and language spoken at home.
whyUseIt: Census data is the foundation of electorate profiling. TableBuilder lets you extract demographic data at the electorate, suburb, or postcode level — giving campaigns the information they need to understand who lives in their electorate and what they might care about.
funnelPosition: Strategic planning — informing targeting and messaging
marketerWorkflow: Build a demographic profile of your electorate, compare to neighbouring electorates and national averages, identify distinctive features, use to inform messaging and targeting strategy
managerExpects: A comprehensive electorate demographic profile that identifies the key voter segments and their likely concerns

vocabulary.definition: Statistical Areas — the geographic building blocks used by the ABS for data reporting
Australia's five-yearly count of every person in the country — the most comprehensive demographic dataset available

beginnerMistakes: Using national averages when electorate-level data tells a very different story
Not accounting for population changes between Census years when comparing data
